documents.text: From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 4/8/2026 17:14:47 PDT Page 1 of 9 From: Jiashou Xu To: A Plus Home Health Email: drxu@lvsurgical.com Email: Phone: (702) 258-7788 Phone: Fax: +1 702-442-8641 Fax: +1 702-342-8398 Patient Name: Daisy Mackey Phone: 702-267-7146 DOB: 09/25/1947 Address 945 Midnight View Ave Age: 78 Henderson, NV 89015 Sex Female Mail Address Same as address SSN Not on file Primary Insurance Secondary Insurance Company: Medicare NV Company: Tricare For Life Plan: Plan: Group #: Group #: Policy #: 3CN1DF4PW26 Policy #: 00572879802 Subscriber: Daisy Mackey Subscriber: Daisy Mackey Instructions: Good afternoon, We would like to refer our patient over for Skilled nursing for wound care. Please see attached order and let us know If you have any questions or concerns. Thank you Reason for referral: Skilled nurse Diagnoses: ICD-10 Code Description C67.1 Malignant neoplasm of dome of bladder K51.313 Ulcerative (chronic) rectosigmoiditis with fistula Afand Provider: Jiashou Xu Date: 04/08/26 05:14 PM From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 4/8/2026 17:14:47 PDT Page 2 of 9 Patient: Daisy Mackey DOB: 09/25/1947 Sex: F Provider: Arthur Fusco Visit: 04/06/2026 10:45AM Chart: MADA07CC273B Chief Complaint: Follow-up Appointment Medications & Allergies: Allergy Reaction iodine penicillin G Subjective: History of present illness: Patient reports drainage from her lower midline incision in 2 areas. Patient was seen at the emergency room and was placed on antibiotics. No imaging has been done. Patient undergoing immunotherapy as per medical oncology for her metastatic squamous cell carcinoma of the bladder. Tolerating diet and colostomy is functioning Objective: System Vitals 04/06/26 Temperature 98.00 f BMI 20.05 kg/m2 Height 69.00 In Weight 135.80 lbs Abdomen soft nontender nondistended. Colostomy functioning. Area of midline incision with 1 area draining purulent material, no succus. This was probed with a Q-tip and noted to go into approximately 8 to 9 cm. Wound packed with plain gauze. Assessment: Status post exam under anesthesia, diagnostic laparoscopy, robotic mobilization of left colon with sigmoid colon with robotic lyse adhesions, exploratory laparotomy with takedown of colovesical and enterovesical fistulas and segmental small bowel resection, repair of bladder dome and biopsy of bladder proximal diverging loop sigmoid colostomy in a patient with squamous cell carcinoma of the bladder with invasion to the small bowel with metastatic disease noted on immunotherapy Plan: Discussed findings with both the patient and patient's son at length. Continue local wound care daily at home with plain gauze packing. Will get CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with contrast to evaluate this wound/possible fistula. Return to clinic after the CT scan is completed. Instructions given E-signed by Arthur Fusco on 04/06/2026 11:07AM PDT Powered by [Page 1 of 1] This page was generated at 04/06/2026 11:07AM PDT DrChrono From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 4/8/2026 17:14:47 PDT Page 3 of 9 A+PLUS A PLUS HOME HEALTH SYSTEMS Referral HOMEHEALTHSTSTENS.LI 5670 W Flamingo Rd, Ste D, Las Vegas NV 89103 Ph: (702) 720-7929 1 Fax: (702) 342-8398 Form Date: 04/08/2026 Referral Source Information: Referring Agency: Las Vegas Surgical Phone: 702-258-7786 Physician Name: Dr Arthur Fusco Patient Information: 702-267-7146, Patient Name: Daisy Mackey Phone: 702-376-6128 Patient Address: 945 Midnight View Ave, Henderson, NV Primary Diagnosis (ICD 10): Malignant neoplasm of dome of bladder, Ulcerative (chronic) rectosigmoiditis with fistula Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply): C67.1, Skilled Nursing K51.313 Medication Management IV Antibiotics XWound Care Skilled Monitoring Diabetic Care Skilled nursing to perform daily Pack wound with iodoform gauze, ensuring loose Others: packing to fill the wound bed without overpacking. Cleanse wound with normal Physical Therapy saline. Wound measures approximately 10 cm depth X 3 cm width. Cover with appropriate sterile secondary dressing (e.g., dry gauze or ABD pad) Occupational Therapy Social Worker Skilled Physical Therapy evaluation and treatment for patient with abdominal surgical incision and functional mobility limitations Home Health Aide Frequency: 2 times per week for 4 weeks, then reassess. Other Pertinent Information: MD Signature And Please FAX this form to (702) 342-8398 Further Instructions: When we call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the patient. If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form. Patient demographic information (including insurance info) - Recent H&P or recent progress notes Medication List THANK YOU FOR YOUR REFERRAL!!! Plan of Treatment Plan Description Planned Type Date From LV Surgery Associate 17024428641 4/8/2026 17:14:47 PDT Page 8 of 9 Plan Description Planned Type Date Plan Patient does have follow-up with urology. Patient will have a CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with - Note contrast/cystogram to evaluate for possible bladder leak postsurgery. If there is no leak from her bladder or her Foley catheter and JP drain can be removed. Patient does have follow-up with urology also. Risk benefits alternative therapy explained to both the patient and patient's son at length who appeared to understand all these issues and agreed. Patient will need treatment for her squamous cell carcinoma of her bladder Plan CT scan was reviewed with both the patient and patient's son at length. Patient has follow-up with them - Note urology to decide about removing Foley catheter. Patient has follow-up with medical oncology due to her immunotherapy for bladder carcinoma. Return to clinic in 3 to 4 weeks for follow-up. Instructions given Plan It was explained to the patient and patient's son that most likely she will need to keep her diverging loop - Note colostomy since she has a lot of bladder carcinoma noted at the time of surgery. Patient was cleared to start back on her immunotherapy as per medical oncology. Instructions given. Return to clinic as needed Plan Patient reports noting food in her urine as well as blood. Patient will be scheduled for a CT scan of the - Note abdomen pelvis with contrast. Patient is following up with urology for possible cystoscopy. This is all explained at length to both the patient and patient's son who appeared to understand all these issues. Patient will return to the office after getting her CT scan performed Plan Discussed palliative care with patient's son at length. Discussed possible need for bilateral nephrostomy tubes - Note by IR. Discouraged any further surgical intervention due to disease progression. Return to clinic as needed. Follow-up with urology and medical oncology Plan Discussed findings with both the patient and patient's son at length. Continue local wound care daily at home - Note with plain gauze packing. Will get CT scan of the abdomen pelvis with contrast to evaluate this wound/possible fistula. Return to clinic after the CT scan is completed.
